label: SQLite

schema_introspection.tables: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name

schema_detail_introspection.command: python3 main.py --command schema_detail --db example.db --table users --format json
schema_detail_introspection.create_sql: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?

notes: Use parameter binding for limit and offset values when the driver supports it.
SQLite uses dynamic typing with storage classes; keep generated DDL conservative.

joins.inner: SELECT ... FROM left_table INNER JOIN right_table ON ...
joins.left: SELECT ... FROM left_table LEFT JOIN right_table ON ...
joins.cross: SELECT ... FROM left_table CROSS JOIN right_table
notes: Prefer explicit JOIN syntax over comma joins.
Foreign key enforcement depends on PRAGMA foreign_keys.

upsert.template: INSERT INTO table_name (...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (...) DO UPDATE SET ...

safety_notes: CoQuery treats SQLite as the working baseline.
CoQuery write commands require explicit SQL plus --write.
